spec_match.rationale: The live preview is faked with RGBA, and the generated CSS showcases the older oklch(from...) Relative Color Syntax instead of the milestone's alpha() function.

interactive.rationale: Good interactive controls for stops, base color, and stop count, updating the preview live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb and demo focus on oklch(from...) Relative Color Syntax rather than the simpler alpha() function which is the core of this milestone feature.

edge_cases.rationale: Faking the gradient with JS RGBA avoids dealing with native browser support and fallbacks.

references.rationale: The parent index.html cites the ChromeStatus link, though it is missing from this concept page's see also section.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to the design system layout and styles.

openQuestions.title: Switch from oklch(from...) to native alpha() function
openQuestions.detail: The demo and generated CSS currently showcase the older oklch(from...) Relative Color Syntax. Since this feature is about the new alpha() function, the builder should generate alpha(var(--color), N%) which is the actual milestone feature, and apply it natively in the preview.

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus link to see also
openQuestions.detail: Add the direct chromestatus.com/feature/5070160203481088 link to the 'see also' section of this concept page for better reference.
